div.p: Logic: Only the owner thread modifies bottom. A Relaxed load is sufficient as there are no data races on this specific write from the perspective of the owner.
Logic: The Acquire ordering is mandatory here to synchronize with any thieves that successfully executed a CAS on top. It ensures we see the most up-to-date head of the queue.

p.em: Calculates effective size and compares to circular buffer boundary.

ol.li: Map old elements [t, b) to new_buffer.

div.p: Invariants: Prevents the processor/compiler from reordering the element write (Step 5) after the bottom increment (Step 7). Thieves must not see the new index until data is valid.
Logic: Increments the producer index. Because of the Release fence in Step 6, this store "publishes" the new item to all threads.

tr.td: Owned by current thread; no external race.
Non-atomic write protected by Release fence.
Ensure Write visibility before Index update.
Standard increment; ordering provided by fence.

div.p: Array Layout (Modulo Capacity): text [ t ] -> [ ] -> [ ] -> [ b ] | | (Head) (Next Slot)
